specification.topic-statement: The spinner layer renders an animated single-row braille character indicator that advances on a time-based schedule while the agent awaits a response.

scope.in-scope: Spinner frame management, time-based frame advancement, visibility control, single-row rendering, and integration with the REPL event loop for animation timing.
scope.boundary: This topic provides three callbacks: visibility, height, and render.
This topic reads and writes a spinner state record owned by the agent. The agent's state machine (IDLE / WAITING_FOR_LLM / EXECUTING_TOOL / dead) controls the visibility flag within that record.
The event loop reads CLOCK_MONOTONIC time and calls the time-based advance operation after each select() iteration. If advance occurs, the loop triggers a full frame re-render externally.

field.name: last_advance_ms
field.note: CLOCK_MONOTONIC timestamp in milliseconds of last advance

behavior.frame-retrieval: Returns the braille character at position (frame_index modulo 10) in the frame sequence. The modulo ensures the index is always valid regardless of the raw frame_index value.
behavior.manual-frame-advance: Increments frame_index by 1, then applies modulo 10. Frame 9 wraps back to frame 0.
behavior.time-based-frame-advance:
Computes elapsed time as (now_ms − last_advance_ms). If elapsed is greater than or equal to 120, the frame is advanced and last_advance_ms is updated to now_ms; the operation returns true. Otherwise no state change occurs and the operation returns false.
If now_ms is less than last_advance_ms (e.g., after a clock adjustment), elapsed is negative and no advance occurs. The spinner stalls until the clock catches up.
behavior.visibility-check:
Returns the boolean visible flag from the spinner state.
Visibility is set to true when the agent transitions from IDLE to WAITING_FOR_LLM. It is set to false when the agent transitions back to IDLE. During frame rendering, if the agent is dead, the visible flag is forced to false regardless of the stored value. If the agent is in WAITING_FOR_LLM or EXECUTING_TOOL state, it is forced to true.
behavior.height-reporting: Always returns 1. The spinner occupies exactly one terminal row.
behavior.rendering:
The width, start_row, and row_count parameters are ignored.
The current braille frame character (3 bytes UTF-8) is appended, followed by the literal text " Waiting for response..." (24 bytes), followed by an ANSI erase-to-end-of-line sequence and CRLF (5 bytes). No color sequences are applied. The content is the same for every frame except the leading braille character.
behavior.animation-loop-integration:
When the spinner is visible, the REPL event loop's select() call times out after 80 milliseconds. After each iteration, the current CLOCK_MONOTONIC time is read in milliseconds and passed to the time-based advance operation. If the operation returns true (at least 120 milliseconds have elapsed since the last advance), a full frame re-render is triggered. This produces approximately 8 animation frames per second.
The select() timeout (80 ms) is shorter than the advance threshold (120 ms). The spinner does not advance on every timeout; it advances only when the 120 ms threshold is crossed. The shorter timeout provides timely checking without causing spurious advances.
